records.display_name: Amtrak East River Tunnel rehabilitation

description: Amtrak rehabilitation work in the East River tunnels to repair damage caused by Superstorm Sandy. Two tunnels require significant work, one tunnel removed from service at a time for approximately 30 months.
Installation of signal houses, traction, and signal power ductbanks and manholes; installation of cable bridge; and a construction work area
Rehabilitation work to East River Tunnels 1 and 2 at Sunnyside Yard in Queens, NY, including installation of signal houses, traction and signal power duct banks and manholes, and a cable bridge
Amtrak project that will shut down one of four tubes; LIRR modifies schedules to accommodate this work
Amtrak rehabilitation work to East River Tunnels 1 and 2 at Sunnyside Yard in Queens, requiring permanent and temporary easements from LIRR.
Large-scale rehabilitation of two East River tunnel tubes damaged by Superstorm Sandy, utilizing a full shutdown over a three-year period
Rehabilitation of the Amtrak East River Tunnel, supported during the March 23, 2026 timetable.

records.display_name: Hall Interlocking Expansion Project

description: Part of JCI Phase 2. A new rail bridge through the center of Jamaica's Hall Interlocking to accommodate two new parallel train routes to increase capacity east of the station.
Hall Interlocking expansion project supported during the March 4, 2024 - May 19, 2024 timetable period.
Expansion of Hall Interlocking

Part of the multi-phase Jamaica Capacity Improvements Program. Constructing a new 280-foot two-track railroad bridge over 150 Street and Atlantic Branch Track 1 using innovative bridge launching techniques. Will create two new parallel routes east of Jamaica Station, eventually becoming Montauk Branch Track 2 and Main Line Track 2.
Expansion of Hall Interlocking.
Projects supported during this timetable to improve state of good repair and reliability

records.display_name: November 10 Timetable - Additional Ronkonkoma Train

payload.description: An additional pre-PM peak train will operate daily from Penn Station to Ronkonkoma. Beginning November 10, departing Penn Station at 3:20pm, making stops at Jamaica, Hicksville, Bethpage, Farmingdale, Wyandanch, Deer Park, Brentwood, Central Islip and Ronkonkoma.
